← the Atlas

Kaval sessions — switch daemons like tmux

feature · seedling ·proposed ·JUN 14, 2026

A direction reframe for R-2. Instead of per-tile remote terminals (a grid that mixes hosts), treat a kaval as a daemon you dial — local over a unix socket, remote over ssh — and switch between kavals like tmux sessions. Switching is the adoption R-4 already shipped; remote becomes a transport, not a feature. Prototype the ssh reach in kaval-tui first.

Contents

  • The model — dial a kaval, switch like tmux
  • Prototype the reach in kaval-tui first
  • Trade-off & status

A reframe of remote-terminals’s R-2, proposed now kaval ships. Old R-2 made remote a per-tile property — every terminal carries a location, the grid mixes hosts, host-knowledge smears onto ~5 surfaces. This proposes the opposite: a kaval is a daemon you dial, and Kolu switches between kavals like tmux sessions — local over a unix socket, remote over ssh, one kaval in view at a time.

kolu⬣ work▾srv ● · client ● · kaval ●
switch kaval
⬣ worklocal · 3 terminalscurrent
⬣ scratchlocal · 1 terminal
⬣ prodsshnix@prod · dial to attach
+ Add a host…
↳ the canvas shows work's terminals — switch and the whole grid swaps

The conflation it dissolves: old R-2 fused “more than one kaval can exist” (real, valuable) with “tiles mix hosts” (what made it heavy). Keep the first, drop the second.

The model — dial a kaval, switch like tmux

kolu-server already keys its pty-host endpoint + status by hostId (a Map, instantiated as one — local — today). This just instantiates more keys: a registry of kaval endpoints, each reached by a driver — unix socket (local, shipped) or ssh stdio (HostSession, R-2’s only new transport). A current pointer picks which kaval the canvas renders.

Switching is cheap because it is adoption you already shipped: adoptOrEnsure (B3.3) runs at boot against local; here it runs on demand against the chosen endpoint. Switch away and the kaval survives — the client just detaches. So host-knowledge collapses to one ambient pointer instead of smearing across the UI:

per-tile location (old R-2)per-kaval switch (this)
“which host” livesevery record + ~5 surfacesone current kaval pointer
the gridmixes hostsone kaval in view
persistencelocation threaded per recordper-kaval session + endpoint registry
the switch—adoptOrEnsure — already shipped
remote is…a per-terminal kinda transport (the ssh driver)

Multiple local kavals fall out for free — kaval is already namespaced per kolu-server port (kaval-<port>/, #1313 ), so several coexist today; the new capability is one server switching among them (isolation: a daemon per project).

Prototype the reach in kaval-tui first

kaval-tui is the minimal client — no switcher, no chips, it just dials a socket and drives a PTY. Teaching it to dial a remote kaval isolates the transport from the UI, and because kaval-tui and kolu-server dial the same socket through the same client, the spike is the production sshDriver, not a throwaway.

kaval-tui · ssh nix@prod → kaval.sock (stdioLink)
$ kaval-tui --host nix@prod list
↳ ssh nix@prod · dialing kaval.sock over stdio … connected
 
ID PID IDLE CMD CWD
a18c…9ff 4471 2m pnpm build --watch ~/app
7b2e…0d4 4502 8s zsh ~/app
 
$ kaval-tui --host nix@prod attach a18c…9ff
↳ snapshot restored — 642 lines · remote PTY 4471 unchanged
▸ build: 1 warning in 3.2s — watching…
~. detach · the remote kaval keeps running

Split remote into its two hard parts and prove the unknown one first:

  • Reach — dial a remote kaval’s socket over an ssh stdioLink (HostSession). Never actually run yet. Prove it first, against a kaval you hand-start on the box — fewest moving parts.
  • Provision — nix copy --derivation the kaval closure, realise, run (provisionAgent). Well-trodden (how odu ships odu-runner, drishti its agent) — automate after reach works.

The spike is on the critical path either way

Per-tile or per-kaval, the ssh-reach transport is shared by both and is R-2’s biggest unknown. Proving it in kaval-tui de-risks the part both models need — and lets real feedback settle the granularity before any switcher UI is designed. So “prototype in kaval-tui first” is the right first step even though the model above isn’t accepted yet.

Trade-off & status

You give up the mixed grid — a local editor tile beside a prod-ssh tile in one saved layout. Right trade for v1: it deletes R-2’s dominant complexity, and it is recoverable — the TerminalLocation discriminator ({ kind: "local" }) still lives at the getTerminalBackendFor seam, so a later mixed-grid feature threads location additively, on a working switch. Open for the spike to answer:

  • Does one-kaval-at-a-time chafe, or is tmux-style switching enough?
  • Does the existing KAVAL rail column generalize to the switcher, and does a remote kaval’s DisconnectedOverlay belong on the whole canvas, not per-tile?
  • Endpoint registry: how are kavals named/added (ssh config? explicit list?), and does the per-kaval session partition compose with the restore-card flow?

Status — proposed. Gate: the kaval-tui ssh-reach spike (reach before provision). Lands clean and one-kaval-at-a-time holds → this supersedes per-tile R-2 in remote-terminals and flips to accepted. Surfaces a real need for the mixed grid → per-tile returns, transport already proven. Either way the spike is the next concrete step.
